description:
Perform a rolling window regression analysis to investigate the stability of inflation persistence.

The 'usa.gdt' dataset is loaded (US quarterly data). You must:
1. Write and execute a Gretl script to run a rolling OLS regression of 'inf' (Inflation) on 'const' and 'inf(-1)' (Lagged Inflation).
2. Use a rolling window size of 24 observations (6 years).
3. Iterate from observation 25 to the end of the sample.
4. In each iteration, store the slope coefficient of 'inf(-1)' and its standard error.
5. Save these results to a CSV file at '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/rolling_results.csv' with headers (e.g., 'coeff', 'se').
6. Generate a plot of the slope coefficient series and save it to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/persistence_plot.png'.
7. Save your script as '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/run_rolling.inp'.
